title: Green routing for active modes of transport
summary: A demonstrator routing engine for walking and cycling that scores route options by air quality, shade, and green-space exposure using Copernicus data.
narrative:
## What it does
This demonstrator suggests walking and cycling routes between two points that minimise exposure to poor air quality and maximise shade/green coverage, drawing on CAMS air quality fields and CLMS land cover (tree canopy, urban green space) alongside standard road/path network data.

## Why it matters
Active-mode routing tools today mostly optimise for distance or elevation. Layering in EO-derived environmental exposure gives cities and routing-app providers a way to promote healthier, more pleasant active travel - relevant to urban mobility strategies and public health co-benefits of cycling/walking investment.

## Status
This is a placeholder entry describing a demonstrator concept; no live instance is published yet.
